description_long: This is a classic hammer featuring a robust, metallic head and a straight, wooden handle. The head is composed of two distinct ends: one flat, cylindrical striking face for driving nails and a sharply tapered, pointed peen for prying or demolition tasks. The metallic head is a matte gray, suggesting it is made of steel, and is securely mounted onto the handle. The handle is a medium brown wood, smooth and cylindrical, providing a comfortable grip for the user. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding, decorative elements, or additional attachments. The hammer appears clean and well-maintained, with no visible signs of wear or damage.
description: A traditional hammer with a steel head featuring a flat striking face and a pointed peen, mounted on a smooth, straight wooden handle. The tool is clean, functional, and unadorned.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the hammer, highlighting the flat striking face and the pointed peen on the head.
description_view_1: Narrow end-on view showing the circular cross-section of the head and the slim profile of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the flat striking face and pointed peen, with the handle fully visible.
description_view_3: Rear end-on view showing the back of the hammer head and the alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle, cylindrical, smooth surface, no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Cross-peen hammer head with a flat striking face on one end and a sharply pointed peen on the other, made of steel.
